transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: It is late summer in Bellwether: warm afternoons, cooler evenings, haze and intermittent rain.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The bus leaves you at Bellwether just after nine. Its engine noise thins along the road until the village is quiet again.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: For now, there is the road ahead, your suitcase at your feet, and Eleanor's key in your pocket.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The road beyond the bus stop has begun to feel less like the way out and more like one edge of Bellwether.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm: Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road is busy with passing traffic.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You pick up a few ordinary household necessities. It is a small thing, but carrying them back makes your stay feel less temporary.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is pausing to look in one of the shop windows.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, approaching Bellwether along the outer road.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is pausing to look in one of the shop windows.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, approaching Bellwether along the outer road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is pausing to look in one of the shop windows.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Delivery van is nearby, rearranging parcels for the next delivery.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You read the notices instead of merely glancing at them: a jumble sale, a lost glove, choir practice, someone offering ladder repairs.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: A few surnames recur across unrelated notices. Bellwether's clubs, errands, and favours seem to run through the same small circle of households.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.label: Take a Slow Walk Around the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You follow the worn paths around the green, learning which gate sticks and where the ground stays damp.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The green and the bell: From one side of the oak the church is hidden, but its bell carries across the whole green with surprising clarity.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Recent activity at Village Green: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.label: Walk down to the riverside path

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Riverside Path.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You follow the river beneath alder branches, learning where the bank is firm and where the meadow path disappears after rain.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: A narrow path follows a shallow river below the village. Alders lean over dark water and a footbridge joins the meadow paths.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Harvest, preserving and late-summer maintenance. The road is quiet between scheduled buses. Swallows gathering and wasps around fallen fruit.

transcript.label: Follow the meadow path to the station

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Bellwether Halt.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: mist, 20°C, with still wind.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait without needing a train. The platform gathers distant sounds from fields, rails and the village beyond the meadow.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The timetable is sparse enough to memorize. Missing one train here can change the shape of an entire day.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Signs of regular waiting: Wear beneath the shelter suggests the same few positions are occupied often despite the sparse service.

transcript.label: Follow the meadow path to the river

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Riverside Path.

transcript.label: Watch the Water for a While

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The river looks slow until you fix your attention on leaves and foam moving beneath the footbridge.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The day's movement is beginning to turn homeward.

transcript.label: Climb back to the village green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.label: Sit on the Bench and Watch the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You sit beneath the old oak. People cross the green on errands that make sense to them and, slowly, begin to make sense to you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: light rain, 20°C, with gentle wind.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The green no longer feels like a landmark on a map. You are beginning to recognize its rhythms.

transcript.text: Morning. How are you?

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Hello, dear. How are you getting on?

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: How has your day been?

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Forgive me, dear, my mind wandered. Go on.

transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is chatting with someone about the morning weather.

transcript.text: You can now place several houses by their gates, gardens, and the sounds that come from behind them.

transcript.text: Stone cottages lean toward the narrow road. Shop windows, garden gates and the turn toward the church give the village its daily rhythm.

transcript.speaker: Nearby
transcript.text: You catch part of a conversation.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: Morning. You're out early.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: So are you. I thought I'd take the air while it's quiet.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: It won't stay quiet for long.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: No. It never seems to, once everyone gets moving.
